name: Git Workflow
description: Git workflow rules for conventional commits, branch naming, PR descriptions, squash merging, and changelog generation
author: StartUpp AI IDE
rules: Use Conventional Commits format: type(scope): description -- types are feat, fix, docs, style, refactor, perf, test, build, ci, chore, revert
Keep commit subjects under 72 characters; use the body for detailed explanations and motivation
Branch names follow the pattern: type/short-description (e.g., feat/user-auth, fix/login-redirect, chore/update-deps)
Create feature branches from the main branch; never commit directly to main or develop
Write PR descriptions with: Summary (what and why), Changes (bullet list), Testing (how to verify), and Breaking Changes sections
Squash merge feature branches into main to keep a clean linear history; delete the branch after merge
Use interactive rebase to clean up commit history before opening a PR -- squash fixups, reword unclear messages
Tag releases with semantic versioning (v1.2.3) and generate changelogs from conventional commit messages
Never force-push to shared branches (main, develop, release/*); only force-push to your own feature branches
Reference issue numbers in commits and PR descriptions (e.g., fixes #123, closes #456) for automatic issue linking

promptTemplates.name: Write Commit Message
promptTemplates.description: Generate a conventional commit message for changes
promptTemplates.template: Write a conventional commit message for the following changes: {{changes}}. Use the correct type (feat/fix/refactor/etc.), include a scope if applicable, keep the subject under 72 characters, and add a body explaining the motivation if the change is non-trivial.

promptTemplates.name: Write PR Description
promptTemplates.template: Write a pull request description for a branch that {{branchPurpose}}. Include: a Summary section explaining what and why, a Changes section with a bullet list of modifications, a Testing section describing how to verify the changes, and a Breaking Changes section if applicable.

conventions:
Git workflow conventions:
- main branch is always deployable; protect it with required PR reviews and CI checks
- Feature branches are short-lived: merge within a few days, not weeks
- Commit frequently with small, focused changes; each commit should be independently meaningful
- Use git hooks (Husky + lint-staged) to enforce linting and commit message format
- Release branches (release/v1.2.0) are cut from main when preparing a release
- Hotfix branches (hotfix/critical-bug) branch from the latest release tag and merge to both main and the release branch
- Keep .gitignore comprehensive: node_modules, build output, IDE files, OS files, .env
